name: persona-plugin
description: Run market research with simulated persona panels — review marketing assets, run interviews / surveys / focus groups / A-B tests / Van Westendorp / conjoint / MaxDiff / TURF / brand tracking / ethnographic / grounded theory and more. Personas live as .md files in a local .personas/ folder; build them via interview, bulk import, distillation from Slack / X / web research, or statistical synthesis from survey data (Gaussian copula).

interface.shortDescription: Persona panels for market research — review, interview, survey, A/B test, price, brand-track, and more.
interface.longDescription: Run market research with simulated persona panels. Build personas via interview, bulk import, distillation from Slack / X / web, batch generation of many unique personas from a single description, or statistical synthesis from survey data via Gaussian copula (persona-copula). Then review assets (persona-review), optimize an asset against the panel in a stopping-aware loop using the goal feature (persona-goal), rehearse a live conversation where a persona plays the counterpart — journalist interview, sales pitch, meeting with your boss (persona-roleplay), ask one persona a question (persona-ask), fuse many personas' independent answers into one joint answer (persona-of-thought), or run a full study (persona-research dispatches to interview / survey / focus group / council / user test / presentation feedback / A/B test / concept test / Van Westendorp / conjoint / MaxDiff / TURF / brand tracking / social listening / ethnographic / diary study / grounded theory / town simulation / Hacker News simulation / tree test / JTBD interview). References-and-confidence grounding in every response. Persona simulations are best where real research isn't possible — never a substitute for talking to actual customers.
defaultPrompt: Create a persona for my target customer
Review this landing-page copy with the personas
Run a Van Westendorp on this product
What would my personas think of this pricing page
